title: Tanacetum pallens
intro: Tanacetum pallens, also known as the pale pyrethrum or white pyrethrum, is a flowering plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties, which make it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum pallens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tanacetum pallens is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and infections.
taxonomy: Tanacetum pallens is a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread flowering plant families, with over 23,000 species. The genus Tanacetum is a group of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, comprising about 60 to 100 species. Tanacetum pallens is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and is known for its white or pale yellow flowers. The plant is often classified as a shrubby perennial with a woody base and a spreading habit.
